arguments.task: Open official Mailchimp developer pages and extract the following from first-party content only: one-line product/API description, all documented authentication methods for the Marketing API and related official APIs if listed on auth docs, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), breadth of available APIs/endpoints, any official MCP/server mention, rate limit notes, and evidence URLs. Also open official Mailchimp pricing or plan pages and determine whether developer API access is self-serve free, trial, paid-plan-required, admin-approval-required, or partner/contact-sales gated. Only use live canonical Mailchimp pages that load and contain supporting text. Return concise findings with exact page URLs used.

input.thought: Start browser-based review of official Mailchimp developer and pricing pages.

raw_result.description: Mailchimp provides marketing, transactional email, and commerce APIs for campaigns, audiences, automations, and ecommerce workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Marketing API docs state you can connect with OAuth 2 or generate an API key. Transactional API fundamentals indicate authentication via an API key and also support SMTP for sending transactional email; the SMTP credential flow is mapped to other because it is not one of the standard listed auth enums.
raw_result.gating_reason: Mailchimp’s public pricing page shows a Free marketing plan, and the developer site publicly documents self-serve API access for the Marketing API without any contact-sales or admin approval requirement in the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Mailchimp’s developer site exposes multiple public API families: the Marketing API is REST with broad resources such as campaigns, audiences/lists, reports, automations, and more; Transactional covers transactional email endpoints; Open Commerce is presented as a GraphQL commerce API.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Mailchimp MCP or Model Context Protocol server was identified in the gathered first-party pages.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Marketing API fundamentals note a limit of 10 simultaneous connections and a 120-second stream timeout in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Mailchimp maintains separate official developer tracks for Marketing, Transactional, and Open Commerce APIs, which is a strong signal of meaningful agent-buildable surface area.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The gathered evidence supports most fields, but the exact access-tier conclusion for API usage on the Free plan and the precise auth classification for Open Commerce were not explicitly confirmed from a first-party page excerpt in the collected evidence. These structured fields were inferred from surrounding official pages and should be human-checked.
